meta.title: Our Therapeutic Games — Autigames
meta.description: Explore Autigames therapeutic games: Emotion Map, Calm Lab, What Face Is That? and Think With Me. Made for children with ASD.

hero.title: Games that make
hero.description: Each game is a doorway to a skill. Discover the launched titles and those on the way.

cta.title: Ready to get started?
cta.subtitle: Download the app for free and explore the games with your child.

game.ctaComingSoonSubtitle: This game is in development. Download the app to be notified when it launches.

mapa-das-emocoes.name: Emotion Map
mapa-das-emocoes.shortDescription: Learn to identify and name emotions in everyday situations.
mapa-das-emocoes.description: The Emotion Map guides children through everyday scenarios where they need to identify how each character is feeling. Through facial expressions, body language, and context, children develop emotional vocabulary and the ability to recognize emotions in themselves and others.
mapa-das-emocoes.step0desc: The child views a scenario with characters in everyday situations — school, home, park.
mapa-das-emocoes.step1desc: Using visual and contextual clues, the child chooses which emotion the character is feeling.
mapa-das-emocoes.step2desc: The game explains simply why that emotion makes sense in that context, reinforcing the learning.

laboratorio-da-calma.name: Calm Lab
laboratorio-da-calma.shortDescription: Emotional regulation techniques presented in a playful and interactive way.
laboratorio-da-calma.description: Calm Lab presents emotional regulation techniques — breathing, mindfulness, and relaxation strategies — in a gamified way. Children learn concrete tools to cope with situations of sensory or emotional overload.
laboratorio-da-calma.step0desc: The child assesses how they are feeling using an interactive emotion thermometer.
laboratorio-da-calma.step1desc: The game presents techniques such as deep breathing, body movement, or mindfulness.
laboratorio-da-calma.step2desc: The child practices the guided technique and the game records which strategies work best for them.

que-cara-e-essa.name: What Face Is That?
que-cara-e-essa.shortDescription: Facial expression recognition training with expressive characters.
que-cara-e-essa.description: What Face Is That? focuses specifically on facial expression recognition — one of the greatest challenges for children with ASD. Through highly expressive 3D characters, children learn to associate micro-expressions with specific emotions.
que-cara-e-essa.step0desc: The character displays a facial expression and the child has a few seconds to observe it carefully.
que-cara-e-essa.step1desc: The child chooses from four options which emotion the character is expressing.
que-cara-e-essa.step2desc: The game highlights the parts of the face that reveal that emotion most — eyebrows, eyes, mouth.

pense-comigo.name: Think With Me
pense-comigo.shortDescription: Interactive social situations to practice reciprocity and perspective-taking.
pense-comigo.description: Think With Me presents complex social situations where children need to understand another character's perspective to make the best decision. The game directly develops Theory of Mind and social reciprocity.
pense-comigo.step0desc: An everyday social situation is presented with two characters interacting.
pense-comigo.step1desc: The child answers what the other character is thinking or feeling in that situation.
pense-comigo.step2desc: Based on the understood perspective, the child chooses the most appropriate social response.

meta.title: About Autigames — Scientific Team and Our Mission
meta.description: Meet the Autigames scientific team: child psychiatrists, a biomedical scientist and a psychologist dedicated to the development of children with ASD through therapeutic games.

hero.title: We believe inclusion starts with
hero.description: Autigames was born from the union of families, healthcare professionals, and technology to build a real bridge for the development of millions of children.

historia.title: Where we come from and why we exist
historia.p1: Autism Spectrum Disorder affects 2.6% of Brazilian children aged 5 to 9. For these children and their families, one of the greatest challenges is not the diagnosis itself — it's day-to-day life. The difficulty of starting a conversation, reading emotions on another person's face, understanding why a situation is funny or sad for others.
historia.p2: Autigames arose from the conviction that well-used technology — with a scientific basis, a clinical perspective, and child-centered design — can be a powerful development tool. Not to replace therapists or the family, but to expand practice opportunities in a safe, predictable, and stimulating environment.
historia.p3: In less than a year since launch, we have already reached over 2,000 downloads, users in more than 16 countries, and recognition as finalists in the Porto Alegre Innovation Award 2025. But the number that matters most is yet to come: the number of children who will find in Autigames a new way to connect with the world.

team.title: The people behind Autigames
team.desc: Healthcare professionals who know first-hand the reality of children with ASD and their families.

tech.text: For over 20 years, LongView has turned challenges into opportunities with digital solutions connecting business, design, and technology. LongView Health is the group's HealthTech unit — with over 300 products created and a solid infrastructure that supports the Autigames platform.

manifesto.quote: "Those who believe in inclusion believe in the future."

context.title: Millions of families worldwide face the same challenge
context.desc: Autism Spectrum Disorder affects around 1 in 36 children. For most of them, socialization is the greatest daily challenge: difficulty starting conversations, understanding emotions, expressing what they feel. Autigames was made to help your family on this journey — with ease, safety, and science.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a0: No. The games have short sessions with a clear beginning, middle, and end. There are no predatory retention mechanics, infinite rewards, or pressure to return.
faq.q1: From what age can my child use it?
faq.a1: The platform is designed for children aged 4 to 12, but it's best to assess individually with your therapist.
faq.a2: No. Autigames is a complementary tool and never replaces the follow-up of a healthcare professional.
faq.a3: The download is free. There is an optional premium plan to unlock all games and tracking features.
faq.a4: Most games work offline. Some progress features require a connection.

por-que-jogos-terapeuticos-funcionam.title: Why therapeutic games work for children with ASD
por-que-jogos-terapeuticos-funcionam.excerpt: Therapeutic gamification is not new — science explains why children with autism respond so well to the game format to develop social and emotional skills.
por-que-jogos-terapeuticos-funcionam.author: Autigames Scientific Team
por-que-jogos-terapeuticos-funcionam.content:
What science says about games and learning

Children learn through play. This truth has been known for decades in developmental psychology — and is increasingly confirmed by neuroscience. For children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD), however, traditional ways of teaching social skills often fail to reproduce the safe, predictable, judgment-free environment that learning requires.

This is where therapeutic games come in with full force.

Predictability as an ally

One of the greatest sources of anxiety for children with ASD is the unpredictability of social interactions. In a real conversation, stimuli are many, simultaneous, and hard to interpret. A digital game, on the other hand, offers:

  • Controlled environment — no unexpected surprises
  • Time to process — no pressure to respond immediately
  • Clear, direct feedback — no ambiguity about what was right or wrong
  • Opportunity for repetition — the same situation can be presented as many times as needed

This predictability is not a limitation of the format — it's exactly what makes games effective for this audience.

Intrinsic motivation and engagement

Neuroscience studies confirm that games activate the brain's reward system in a sustained way. Level progression, positive feedback, and the sense of competence create a flow state that favors learning. For children with ASD, who often show resistance to structured activities, the playful format lowers the entry barrier and increases engagement.

Autigames is designed to leverage exactly these mechanisms — without, however, creating dependency or using predatory retention mechanics. Sessions are short, with a clear beginning, middle, and end.

Skill transfer

The most common concern among parents and therapists is: does what the child learns in the game transfer to real life?

The evidence suggests yes — especially when the game is designed with ecological validity, meaning when the situations presented approach real contexts in the child's life. Autigames works with everyday scenarios (school, family, park) precisely to increase this transfer.

Moreover, when the game is used together with therapeutic follow-up — which we strongly recommend — the therapist can use the game's situations as a starting point for conversations with the child and their family.

A tool, not a solution

It's important to be clear: therapeutic games are complementary tools. They do not replace therapy, medical care, or family support. What they do — and do very well — is offer additional moments of safe practice, at the child's own pace, in the comfort of home.

When parents, therapists, and technology work together, the results are significantly better. It's this synergy that Autigames was created to catalyze.

cta.title: Experience it in practice
cta.subtitle: Download the app for free and see for yourself how therapeutic games can help your child.

meta.title: Privacy Policy — Autigames
meta.description: Privacy policy of the Autigames platform in compliance with data protection laws.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.todoText: This page should be filled with the complete Privacy Policy text prepared by a legal professional specialized in data protection law.
privacy.s1text: Autigames is a Brazilian platform of therapeutic digital games for children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD), developed by LongView Health.
privacy.s2text: We collect only the data necessary for the platform to function and to fulfill the purposes described in this policy. Data includes registration information, app usage data, and children's progress information.
privacy.s3text: Data is used exclusively for: (a) providing platform services; (b) improving the user experience; (c) service-related communication; and (d) fulfilling legal obligations.
privacy.s4text: We do not share children's personal data with third parties for commercial purposes. Data may be shared with technical service providers who act under our instructions and in accordance with this policy.
privacy.s5text: In accordance with data protection law, you have the right to access, correct, delete, or port your data. To exercise these rights, contact contato@autigames.com.br.
privacy.s6text: For questions about this policy or the processing of your data, contact: contato@autigames.com.br

meta.title: Terms of Use — Autigames
meta.description: Terms of use for the Autigames platform.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.todoText: This page should be filled with the complete Terms of Use prepared by a legal professional.
terms.s1text: By using the Autigames platform, you agree to these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any provision, do not use the platform.
terms.s2text: Autigames is a platform of therapeutic digital games aimed at children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). The games are developed with a scientific basis and are intended solely for complementary therapeutic and educational purposes.
terms.s3text: Autigames is a complementary tool and does not replace professional medical, psychological, or therapeutic follow-up. We are not responsible for clinical decisions made based solely on the use of the platform.
terms.s4text: All platform content — games, characters, texts, images, and code — is the property of Autigames and is protected by law. Reproduction without prior written authorization is prohibited.
terms.s5text: For questions about these terms: contato@autigames.com.br

meta.title: Terms of Use & Privacy — Autigames
meta.description: Terms of use and privacy policy for the Autigames platform, compliant with Brazilian data protection law (LGPD).

combined.title: Terms of Use and Privacy Policy
combined.intro:
This document establishes the Terms of Use and Privacy Policy of the Autigames platform, available through a mobile app and/or web environment, owned by AUTIGAMES JOGOS DIGITAIS LTDA, registered under CNPJ nº 60.955.520/0001-80, headquartered at AV. CEL. Lucas de Oliveira 1107, Bairro Mon't Serrat, Porto Alegre – Rio Grande do Sul – CEP 90450-045 (Autigames, Platform or We).

By creating an account, accessing or using the Platform, the user declares that they have read, understood, and fully agree to the terms below.
combined.s1text:
Family Account: account model intended for home use, under the responsibility of an adult.
Professional Account: intended for professionals, institutions, or service providers.
Player Profile: behavioral and progress data without direct personal identification.
Games: interactive experiences made available on the Platform.
Subscription: recurring payment for access to content.
User: any person who uses the Platform.
combined.s2text:
Autigames is a digital platform of interactive games with a socioemotional and educational focus.

Autigames is not a medical, therapeutic, or diagnostic device, does not replace psychological, psychiatric, pedagogical, or clinical care, and does not provide reports or prescriptions.
combined.s3text:
3.1 Family Account
Create a Player Profile with progress tracking
Access to the Emotion Map game
Access to additional games with an active subscription
Access to future updates
The guardian declares they are 18 years of age or older and assumes full responsibility for the use of the Platform.

3.2 Professional Account
Plans with up to 5, 10, or more than 10 profiles
Individual management of Player Profiles
Game-specific, character, and stage configuration
Private notes per profile and session
Notes are for the professional's exclusive use and are not reviewed by Autigames.
combined.s4text:
4.1 Data we do NOT collect
Legal name
CPF, ID, or official documents
Physical address
Biometric or medical data

4.2 Data we collect
Behavioral data generated during use
Progress, choices, and interactions
Technical data and error logs
combined.s5text: Autigames operates in compliance with Brazilian Law nº 13.709/2018 (LGPD), using legal bases such as contract performance, legitimate interest, and consent.
combined.s6text:
Access to certain content requires an active subscription.

Cancellation can be made at any time, taking effect at the end of the current billing cycle, with no proportional refund unless required by law.
combined.s7text: All Platform content is protected by copyright and belongs exclusively to Autigames or its licensors.
combined.s8text: Use of the Platform is at the user's own risk, subject to applicable legal limits.
combined.s9text: We adopt adequate technical and organizational measures to protect data.
combined.s10text: These Terms may be changed at any time. Continued use implies agreement with the updated version.
combined.s11text: Brazilian law applies, with the courts of Porto Alegre – RS designated as the competent jurisdiction.
